mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces borrowing costs for corporations and households, directly boosting equity valuations through lower discount rates and higher expected earnings over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering recession fears that compress equity multiples and drive negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or t-test p-value exceeds 0.05 over 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and economic expansion, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression, or joint growth periods yield negative forward returns > 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects accelerating manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which precedes earnings revisions and drives positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during industrial production accelerations are negative in > 55% of cases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained job creation, reinforcing consumer spending power and corporate profitability expectations, leading to positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth periods show no statistical edge over baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC drops below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A consecutive two-month decline in housing starts signals weakening residential construction demand, which cascades through related sectors and reduces aggregate economic activity, forecasting negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns during housing starts declines are positive in > 60% of instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates real tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, generating negative forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: Joint inflation-rate tightening periods yield positive forward returns > 55% of the time, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and retail sales growth reveals supply chain bottlenecks or inventory imbalances, creating earnings uncertainty that suppresses equity valuations in the short term.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth exceeds RSAFS growth by > 1.5pp
failure_condition: Divergence periods produce positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC/BIC) over single-series baselines.
